claim: Framework-jurisdiction invariants (frameworkScopedInvariantNames) shall run only against a graph whose manifest declares self_hosting=true.
why: Wave 7 docrystallization of the gap named by the Wave 6/prior agent's landed bijection work: ontology.Graph.SelfHosting (internal/ontology/graph.go) is populated at load time by loader.resolveSelfHosting (internal/loader/loader.go), which reads the sibling manifest.json's self_hosting field (default false when absent or unreadable); invariants.AllViolations (internal/invariants/all_violations.go) gates the frameworkScopedInvariantNames set on this flag so those invariants fire ONLY when g.SelfHosting is true -- domains/hotam-spec-self/manifest.json sets {"self_hosting": true} (it IS the framework modeling itself); any other business domain (e.g. domains/hotam-dev/manifest.json, which does not set self_hosting) defaults to false and is correctly exempt from framework-internal plumbing checks that have no bearing on business content. The frameworkScopedInvariantNames set covers the domain/agent/tool-plumbing structural checks that only make sense against the framework's OWN self-model (check_domain_manifest_*, check_domain_director_exists, check_agent_has_*_subdir, check_bijection_r_to_enforcer, check_method_matches_docstring, check_rules_as_data_classification_coherent, check_constituting_not_in_unresolved_conflict). Without this gate, framework-jurisdiction checks would fire as phantom P1 violations against business domains that never claim to model the framework itself. Evidence: internal/loader/loader.go resolveSelfHosting; internal/ontology/graph.go Graph.SelfHosting field; internal/invariants/all_violations.go frameworkScopedInvariantNames set + the `if scoped && !g.SelfHosting { continue }` gate; domains/hotam-spec-self/manifest.json {"self_hosting": true}. ENFORCED 2026-07-13: TestFrameworkScopedInvariantsRunOnlyWhenSelfHosting in internal/invariants/self_hosting_gate_test.go mechanically verifies this claim.
